title: Cross-document consistency repair
candidate_prompt:
You are cross-checking classroom resources against an approved lesson plan. The APPROVED PLAN is the source of truth. You may repair only the worksheet and slide outline. Do not change, reinterpret or “improve” the approved plan.

APPROVED PLAN
Lesson: Year 8 — Evidence for chemical change (60 min, AH17 dry classroom)
Learning intention: Distinguish observations from inferences and use multiple observations to decide whether evidence supports formation of a new substance.
Success criteria:
1. Label statements as observation or inference.
2. Use at least two relevant observations before claiming a chemical change.
3. State when evidence is insufficient.
Sequence: 0–7 retrieval; 7–18 explicit teaching and teacher model; 18–33 groups build an Evidence–Inference–Decision tool; 33–43 groups test it against two cases and revise one rule; 43–54 individual five-mark check; 54–60 exit ticket and A16 handoff.
Worked example: fizzing tablet in water. Observation: bubbles and temperature decrease. Inference: gas may have formed. Decision: observations alone do not identify the gas or prove a new substance; more evidence is needed.
Individual check: five marks—two observations (2), observation/inference distinction (1), evidence-based decision (1), limitation or further evidence (1).
Exit ticket: “One observation is ___; one inference is ___; my decision is ___ because ___; evidence still needed is ___.”
Next lesson: students use the revised tool in A16 to record live reaction evidence.

WORKSHEET DRAFT
Title: Proving a Chemical Reaction
Learning intention: Identify five signs that prove a chemical reaction occurred.
Success criteria: List colour change, bubbles, odour, temperature and light.
Worked example: Fizzing always proves a gas-producing chemical reaction.
Group task: Circle the strongest sign on six case cards. Copy its name into the table.
Individual check (3 marks): Name three signs of chemical reaction.
Exit: Write one sign that proves a reaction.
Teacher note printed at foot: Correct answers—bubbles, colour change, temperature change.

SLIDE OUTLINE DRAFT
S1 title and safety.
S2 learning intention: Know the indicators of chemical reactions.
S3 success: Recall four indicators.
S4–S5 teacher explanation (12 minutes).
S6 practical demonstration: teacher mixes vinegar and bicarbonate in AH17.
S7 students complete worksheet individually (20 minutes).
S8 worked example: melting wax is chemical because it cannot easily be reversed.
S9 group answer reveal.
S10 four-mark exit quiz: list four signs.
S11 next lesson: copy definitions in A16.

TASK
Return exactly these sections:
1. Mismatch ledger — table with columns ID, document/location, mismatch, exact approved-plan evidence, severity.
2. Worksheet repairs — minimal replacement text for every affected worksheet element.
3. Slide repairs — minimal replacement text or instruction for every affected slide.
4. Verification — map the repaired learning intention, success criteria, worked example, timings, individual check, exit ticket and A16 handoff back to the approved plan.
5. Plan integrity statement — explicitly confirm that no approved-plan element was changed.

Keep the response under 1,200 words. Do not add new practical work or new scientific content. Do not include model identity or benchmark commentary.

planted_mismatches.gold: replace proof/indicator framing with observation-inference and multiple-evidence LI
replace five-sign recall with three approved success criteria
replace fizzing-proves claim with approved fizzing-tablet example and limitation
replace circle/copy with build-test-revise Evidence–Inference–Decision tool
replace 3-mark recall with approved 5-mark evidence reasoning check
replace proof statement with approved four-part exit ticket
remove answers from student material; teacher key separate
align exactly with approved LI and three SC
remove wet demonstration; AH17 is dry
remove false irreversibility claim and use approved fizzing example
replace with test/revise phase without student answer leakage
replace four-sign recall with approved exit ticket
replace copy definitions with use revised tool in A16 live evidence recording

forbidden: claim a single sign proves chemical change

source_notes: Synthetic source-of-truth consistency fixture; all authoritative plan facts are supplied in prompt.
